evals.prompt: Socket PR comment says create-adsk has filesystem access and shell access alerts, and recently published. CI socket-security is yellow/red. What should we do before merge?
evals.expected_output: Treat filesystem/shell as monitor for the CLI; recently published as cool-down; do not gut fs/spawn; only block if High classes (malware, install scripts) appear.
assertions: Does not recommend removing filesystem or child_process from create-adsk solely to raise Socket scores
Mentions recently published / cool-down as non-blocking alone
References policy allowlist or SECURITY.md Socket policy

evals.prompt: PR adds a dependency that runs a postinstall script. Socket flags install scripts. Merge?
evals.expected_output: Block merge; replace or remove the dependency; do not grant a silent exception.
assertions: Identifies install scripts as a block-class alert
Recommends removing or replacing the dependency (not ignoring)

evals.prompt: Wire Socket into GitHub Actions for this kit. Secret may not exist yet.
evals.expected_output: Points at socket.yml + SOCKET_SECURITY_API_KEY; notes graceful skip without secret; points at policy allowlist.
assertions: Mentions .github/workflows/socket.yml or equivalent Socket Actions workflow
Does not invent committing the API token into the repo

evals.prompt: Add a new production dependency to packages/create-adsk/package.json.
evals.expected_output: Runs intake checklist: Socket/health check, reject install scripts, exact pin, npm audit.
assertions: Requires exact version pin (no caret) for create-adsk production deps
Mentions checking Socket or supply-chain signals before adding
